ref: When the Son of Man appears, things will be just as they were when Noah lived.
text: La din teen biaŋinba Nowa yoo na, li mun bia tan sii tee nnee min nisaarik Bik jenu yoo.
translated: Nowa is a good person, he is kind and generous. He is always willing to help.

ref: Then they spit in his face and hit him with their fists. Others slapped him
text: Li yoo na, ki bi ji piin ki siin mɔsant u paak, ki boou, ki siab pebitɔ tanpakii,
translated: If you go there, you will see many people, they will greet you, they will give you food to eat.

ref: So when you see all these things happening, you will know the time has almost come.
text: Nnee mun, yoo nba ki i tan laat ki linba na nba kur tuun, yin bann nan n jenu maŋ pere na, li ji kan yukiri.
translated: If you go to the market, you will find many things there, and you will buy them, and you will be happy.

ref: “The Emperor's,” they answered. Then Jesus told them, “Give the Emperor what belongs to him and give God what belongs to God.”
text: Ki bi jiin a “Siisa na yare.” Ki Yisa betib a “Tɔn, i yenn-ii pa man kii teen Siisa linba tee u yar, ŋaan mun-ii bia pa kii teen Yennu linba tee Yennu yar.”
translated: The first person said, “Siisa is a good person.” The second person replied, “No, Siisa is not a good person, but Yennu is a good person.”

ref: The servants came and asked, “Sir, didn't you scatter good seed in your field? Where did these weeds come from?”
text: Ŋanne ki u ŋaatoontunna baar ki tan boiɔ a ‘Chanbaa, diboorŋanii kuukɔɔ kaa ki a daan bur a kpaab na ni-ii? Ŋaan li teen nlee ki dinatimii tan be leŋi?’
translated: Why are you looking for me like this?  Is it because of Chanbaa, the one who stole my things?  I don't know what you want from me.

ref: You have been taught that a man who divorces his wife must write out divorce papers for her.
text: “Bi bia din yeen a ‘Wunba loon ki wun yêt u ŋaapoo, wun turɔ pooyêtu gbouŋ;’
translated: “The white man said, ‘I will give you a gun, and you will use it to hunt;’

text: ŋaan ŋaant man ki i jiinu-ii tee, ‘Nn,’ amii ‘Aaii,’ kimaan li-i tee ki a por siar ki pukin linba na paak, li ji nyii Biitdaanɔ maŋe. Daa jiint leeb pana man (Luk
translated: Then the man said to him, 'No,' I said, 'Yes,' and he said to me, 'Go and see if you can find a place to stay, because I am going to the city of Biitdaanɔ.  And he said to me, 'Go to the city of Luk

ref: The same thing happened to the second and third brothers and finally to all seven of them.
text: Nnee ki li jii nna ki tan baar wunba pukin bannlee na ni, nan wunba pukin banntaa na ni, ki sa gbenn jab bannlore maŋ kur paak.
translated: If you want to go to the market, go with me, I will go with you to the market, and I will give you some money to buy something.

ref: Everyone in town came out to meet Jesus. When they saw him, they begged him to leave their part of the country.
text: Ki doo na niib kura din bite ki saan ki saa took Yisa; ba din lau yoonba, ki bi piin ki barimɔ a wun nyi bi tiŋ ni.
translated: He said to them, "I will not give you anything; I will not give you anything to eat.

ref: Jesus walked on until he saw James and John, the sons of Zebedee. They were in a boat with their father, mending their nets. Jesus asked them to come with him.
text: Wa din nyii leŋ ki saan tɔɔnn po waaminna, ki u la Sebedee nan u waas bannlee, Jems nan u waarɔ Jɔɔnn, ki bi be burgbetir ni ki lik bi janlaant; ki u yiimm.
translated: And then they went to the place where they would meet, where Sebedee was with his people, James and John, and they were all together; and they were all together.
